content.text: Based on the available technical resources and best practices for production Computer Use patterns,
the capability to capture and store complete agent execution sessions—including screenshots, actions, tool calls, prompts, and state changes
—enabling developers to reproduce failures exactly as they occurred, collaborate around a single source of truth, and debug Computer Use agents without needing to reproduce the issue manually.

attrs.alt: Diagram for Recording and replaying sessions for debugging

content.text: "With Replay, you only need to record the bug once! Once the bug is recorded, you can add the replay to Jira and developers can debug it directly without having to reproduce it"
In Computer Use applications this is especially critical because:
— Agent was misled by untrusted content on screen
Hard to reproduce without exact same webpage
— Window moved, same coordinates now wrong
Need exact screenshot + coordinates to debug
Need to see what dialog looked like
Need to see what screenshot showed
: Session replays include user actions, so you can find all sessions where a given action occurred.

Session recording captures:
├── Screenshot sequence (base64 images at each step)
├── Actions taken (click, type, scroll, key)
├── Tool calls (database_query, send_email, etc.)
├── Prompts (user input + system instructions)
├── Responses (model output at each step)
├── State changes (context, variables, memory)
└── Timing (milliseconds between actions)
: "DOM mutations, user interactions, and network requests".

: Get product insights, see what user did before crash
-
: Debug a specific bug during development
: "Install @tracekit/browser and enable replay with one config flag. Recording starts automatically".

Every replay is connected to the backend trace:
├── DOM mutations (UI changes)
├── User interactions (mouse, clicks, scrolls, form inputs)
├── Network requests (API calls with timing)
├── Console logs (errors, warnings, debug messages)
└── Breadcrumbs (user's path through app)
: "See mouse movements, clicks, scrolls, and form interactions. Breadcrumbs show the user's path through your app".

= "records a video with additional metadata":
- Records visual output + event metadata
- Cannot add breakpoints or debug runtime
- Best for product insights

= "replay the runtime exactly as it ran before":
- Records the full runtime state
- Can add breakpoints, print statements, step through code
- Best for reproducing bugs and finding root cause
Seeing what user did before crash
: "Eventually which tool you use will depend on what problem you are trying to solve".

# Python: Configure recording to capture only sessions with errors
Configuration:
    error_triggered: true
    sampling_rate: 1%  # Save storage, focus on what matters
: "Save storage and focus on what matters — only record when errors occur".

Recording rate options:
├── 1% recording: Low cost, only capture interesting failures
├── 10% recording: Moderate cost, catch more edge cases
└── 100% recording: High cost, complete visibility but expensive
: "Adjust sampling to balance insight with cost".

# Python: Configure masking rules for sensitive data
Masking configuration:
    default: true  # Mask sensitive fields by default
    rules:
        - element: "input[type=password]"
          mask: true
        - class: "credit-card-number"
          mask: true
        - data-attribute: "ssn"
          mask: true
: "Sensitive fields are masked by default. Configure masking rules per element, class, or data attribute".

1. Error occurs in production
   ↓
2. Click "View Session" on error
   ↓
3. Replay user experience at that moment
   ↓
4. See exactly what happened:
   ├── Mouse movements
   ├── Clicks and scrolls
   ├── Form interactions
   ├── API calls with timing
   └── Console logs
   ↓
5. Identify root cause in same interface
: "Stop asking users to describe the bug. Replay their session and see exactly what happened. Linked to the trace that shows you why".
"However, as it comes to reproducing graphical user interaction it becomes hard to decide what of events to record and replay"
: "For replaying, you need to have the exact same position of every element at the very same position on the screen".

# Python: Record Computer Use session
class SessionRecorder:
    def __init__(self):
        self.session_data = []
    
    def record_step(self, screenshot, action, prompt, response, tool_call=None):
        """Record one step in Computer Use loop"""
        step = {
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            "screenshot": screenshot,  # base64 image
            "action": action,  # {"type": "click", "coordinate": [342, 156]}
            "prompt": prompt,  # User input
            "response": response,  # Model output
            "tool_call": tool_call,  # Optional (database_query, etc.)
            "latency_ms": time.time() - start_time
        }
        self.session_data.append(step)
    
    def save_session(self, session_id):
        """Save recording to storage"""
        with open(f"sessions/{session_id}.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(self.session_data, f)
    
    def replay_session(self, session_id):
        """Replay recording for debugging"""
        with open(f"sessions/{session_id}.json", "r") as f:
            session_data = json.load(f)
        
        for step in session_data:
            print(f"Step at {step['timestamp']}:")
            print(f"  Action: {step['action']}")
            print(f"  Prompt: {step['prompt']}")
            print(f"  Response: {step['response']}")
            if step['tool_call']:
                print(f"  Tool call: {step['tool_call']}")
            print(f"  Latency: {step['latency_ms']}ms")
            # Optionally display screenshot
            # show_image(step['screenshot'])
: "If a team member records a replay on a Windows laptop in Japan and shares it with you, you will see exactly what they saw".

# Python: Optimal recording settings for Computer Use
recording_config = {
    # Only record when errors occur (save storage)
    "error_triggered": True,
    
    # Sample 1% to balance cost and insight
    "sampling_rate": 0.01,
    
    # Mask sensitive fields by default
    "mask_sensitive_fields": True,
    
    # Record all interactions
    "record": [
        "mouse_movements",
        "clicks",
        "scrolls",
        "form_interactions",
        "api_calls",
        "console_logs"
    ],
    
    # Connect to backend traces
    "link_to_traces": True,
    
    # Include screenshots at each step
    "include_screenshots": True,
    
    # Step frequency (every 100ms)
    "screenshot_interval_ms": 100
}
— Issues that took days to reproduce now identified in minutes
— Clear link between what user did and technical error
No need for users to describe bug
a flight recorder (black box) on an airplane

: it captures everything—altimeter data, control inputs, engine readings, cockpit voice—so when something goes wrong, investigators can replay the exact sequence of events without needing to recreate the crash. For Computer Use agents, the "black box" records screenshots (what agent saw), actions (what agent did), prompts/responses (what agent thought), and tool calls (what agent executed). When the agent crashes or gets stuck, you replay the recording to see exactly where it went wrong, instead of trying to reproduce the bug from scratch.
Session recording captures complete agent execution: screenshots, actions, tool calls, prompts, responses, state, timing
Why important: reproducibility (record bug once, debug without reproducing), collaboration (single source of truth), powerful developer tools
Production tools: FullStory, LogRocket, DataDog, Highlight.run (for product insights)
One-off tools: Bird Eats Bug, Tango.us (for debugging)
Session Replay records video + metadata; Time-Travel Debugging records full runtime for breakpoints/debugging
Configuration: error-triggered (save storage), 1% sampling (balance cost), mask sensitive fields, link to traces
What captured: DOM mutations, user interactions (mouse/clicks/scrolls/forms), network requests, console logs, breadcrumbs
Computer Use specific: record screenshot sequence, actions (click/type/scroll/key), tool calls, prompts/responses, latency
Challenge: for replaying, need exact same position of every element at same position on screen
Benefits: 90% faster debugging (days → minutes), eliminate user miscommunication, better collaboration

"Stop asking users to describe the bug. Replay their session and see exactly what happened. Linked to the trace that shows you why" — the core value of session replay for debugging Computer Use agents.
